question: What colleges are part of PDEA Pune
answer:
Professional Colleges (Major Institutes):
- PDEA College of Engineering
- PDEA Law College
- PDEA College of Pharmacy
- Mahatma Phule Institute of Management & Computer Studies
- Shankarrao Ursal College of Pharmaceutical Sciences & Research Center
- Shankarrao Ursal College of Pharmacy (Diploma)
- Seth Govind Raghunath Sable College of Pharmacy
- Seth Govind Raghunath Sable College of Pharmacy Polytechnic
- PDEA College of Ayurveda & Research Center

These cover engineering, law, pharmacy, management, Ayurveda, and diploma courses.

Regular (Arts, Commerce, Science) Colleges:
- Annasaheb Magar Mahavidyalaya
- Anantrao Pawar College
- Mamasaheb Mohol College
- Prof. Ramkrishna More Arts, Commerce & Science College
- Baburaoji Gholap College
- Shankarrao Bhelke College
- Annasaheb Waghire College of Arts, Science & Commerce

These offer BA, BCom, and BSc programs.

Other Institutions:
- Junior colleges
- Schools (secondary & primary)
- Vocational institutes

Overall, PDEA manages a large number of educational institutions across the Pune district.

question: What professional courses does PDEA offer?
answer: Engineering & technology: B.E. / B.Tech (various branches like Computer, IT, E&TC, Mechanical, etc.), M.E. / M.Tech, B.Arch, MCA (Master of Computer Applications), MBA / PGDM. Law: LL.B (Law degree programs), Integrated Law courses (in some institutes). Pharmacy: B.Pharm (Bachelor of Pharmacy), D.Pharm (Diploma in Pharmacy), M.Pharm (Postgraduate specializations). Management & computer studies: MBA (Master of Business Administration), BBA (in some institutes), Computer application programs. Ayurveda & medical-related: BAMS (Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery), MD (Ayurveda specializations), Ph.D. in Ayurveda. Vocational & other programs: B.Sc Computer Science, M.Sc Computer Science, Diploma courses, Skill-based vocational courses

question: How do I apply for admission to a PDEA college?
answer: Application mode: Online and offline (depending on course and institute). General steps: Visit the official PDEA website or the specific college website, Select the desired college and course, Fill out the online admission/application form, Upload required documents, Submit the application, Participate in merit list or entrance exam process (if applicable), Confirm admission by paying fees and completing verification. Entrance exams required: Engineering: MHT-CET, JEE Main. Pharmacy: MHT-CET (Pharmacy). Law: MH CET Law. Mba: MAH MBA CET, CAT, CMAT. Mca: MAH MCA CET. Ayurveda: NEET (for BAMS). Required documents: 10th and 12th mark sheets, Leaving Certificate (LC), Entrance exam scorecard (if applicable), Aadhaar card / ID proof, Passport size photographs, Caste certificate (if applicable). Summary: To apply for PDEA colleges, students must apply online through the respective college portal, meet eligibility requirements, and complete admission through merit or entrance exams depending on the course.

question: What is the admission process for PDEA colleges?
answer: Overview: Admission to PDEA colleges is done through online applications, merit lists, and entrance exams depending on the course.. Steps: Choose the desired PDEA college and course, Check eligibility criteria for the selected program, Apply online through the college or centralized admission portal, Appear for entrance exam (if required), Check merit list or counseling results, Confirm admission by document verification and fee payment. Entrance exams by course: Engineering: MHT-CET, JEE Main. Pharmacy: MHT-CET (Pharmacy). Law: MH CET Law. Mba: MAH MBA CET, CAT, CMAT, MAT. Mca: MAH MCA CET. Ayurveda: NEET (for BAMS). Required documents: 10th mark sheet, 12th mark sheet, School Leaving Certificate (LC), Entrance exam scorecard (if applicable), Identity proof (Aadhaar or equivalent), Caste certificate (if applicable), Passport-size photographs. Summary: The admission process for PDEA colleges involves applying online, meeting eligibility criteria, qualifying entrance exams where required, and completing document verification and fee payment.

question: Where can I fill the online admission form for PDEA?
answer: Main admission portal: Url: https://www.pdea-admission.org. Description: Centralized online admission portal for many PDEA colleges. Alternative method: Some individual PDEA colleges also have their own admission portals for direct applications.. Application steps: Open the PDEA admission portal, Register using mobile number or email, Select college and course, Fill in personal and academic details, Upload required documents, Submit the application form, Wait for merit list or selection confirmation, Complete admission by paying fees and document verification. Important note: For some courses like Engineering, Pharmacy, MBA, MCA, and Law, admission may also require participation in centralized entrance exam counselling (such as MHT-CET, NEET, or MAH CET).. Summary: You can fill the online admission form for PDEA colleges on the official admission portal https://www.pdea-admission.org or through individual college admission websites.

question: What is the ERP login portal for PDEA admissions?
answer: Erp portal url: https://www.pdea-admission.org. Description: The ERP system for PDEA admissions is integrated into the official PDEA admission portal.. Login sections: Student Login, Institute Login, Online Payment Login, Management (Sanstha) Login. Student login details: Requires: Username, Password, Captcha. Note: Login credentials are provided after successful registration on the admission portal.. Important notes: New users must register before using ERP login, ERP access is enabled after application submission, Used for admission tracking, fee payment, and application status. Summary: The ERP login for PDEA admissions is available through the official admission portal https://www.pdea-admission.org under the Student Login section.

question: What documents are required for admission to PDEA colleges?
answer: Required documents: 10th Marksheet, 12th Marksheet, School Leaving Certificate (LC / TC), Entrance Exam Scorecard (if applicable), Aadhaar Card or Government ID Proof, Passport-size Photographs, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Caste Validity Certificate (if required), Domicile Certificate (if applicable), Income Certificate (if applicable for scholarships), Migration Certificate (for students from other boards/universities). Course specific documents: Engineering: MHT-CET or JEE Main scorecard. Pharmacy: MHT-CET Pharmacy scorecard or relevant entrance proof. Law: MH CET Law scorecard. Mba: MAH MBA CET / CAT / CMAT scorecard. Mca: MAH MCA CET scorecard. Ayurveda: NEET scorecard (for BAMS). Summary: Admission to PDEA colleges requires academic certificates, identity proof, photographs, and entrance exam scorecards (if applicable), along with category and domicile documents when needed.

question: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Tech admission at PDEA College of Engineering?
answer: Basic eligibility: Education: Must have passed 10+2 (HSC) or equivalent. Mandatory subjects: Physics, Mathematics, Chemistry / Biotechnology / Biology / Technical Vocational subject. Minimum marks: General category: At least 50% marks in PCM subjects combined. Reserved category: At least 45% marks in PCM subjects combined. Entrance exam requirement: MHT-CET (Maharashtra Common Entrance Test), JEE Main (Paper I). Selection process: Admission is done through CAP (Centralized Admission Process) based on entrance exam rank/score. Additional routes: Management quota (based on HSC marks and availability of seats). Summary: To get admission in B.Tech at PDEA College of Engineering, students must complete 12th with PCM subjects, secure minimum required marks, and qualify MHT-CET or JEE Main. Admission is mainly through CAP counselling.

question: What is the eligibility for MBA admission at PDEA MPIM?
answer: Institute: Mahatma Phule Institute of Management & Computer Studies (MPIM), Pune. Course: MBA (Master of Business Administration). Basic eligibility: Education: Bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized university. Minimum marks: General category: 50% aggregate marks. Reserved category: 45% aggregate marks (Maharashtra state candidates). Entrance exam requirement: MAH MBA CET, CAT, CMAT, MAT, XAT, ATMA (or equivalent management entrance exams). Selection process: Entrance exam score, Merit list / shortlisting, Counselling or institute-level admission (as applicable). Additional conditions: Final year graduation students may apply provisionally, Admission subject to verification of documents and eligibility norms. Summary: To get MBA admission at PDEA MPIM, candidates must have a bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved category) and a valid score in entrance exams like MAH MBA CET, CAT, CMAT, MAT, or XAT.

question: What is the eligibility for MCA admission at PDEA?
answer: Basic eligibility: Education: Bachelor’s degree in any discipline from a recognized university. Minimum marks: General category: At least 50% aggregate marks. Reserved category maharashtra: At least 45% aggregate marks. Mandatory requirement: Mathematics: Must have Mathematics at 10+2 level or at graduation level (as per admission rules). Entrance exam: MAH MCA CET (primary exam for Maharashtra state admissions). Selection process: Admission is done through CAP (Centralized Admission Process) based on MCA CET rank and merit. Additional notes: Candidates must be Indian nationals, Final year graduation students can also apply provisionally, Admission is confirmed after document verification and seat allotment. Summary: To get MCA admission at PDEA, a candidate must have a bachelor’s degree with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved category) and Mathematics in 10+2 or graduation, along with a valid MAH MCA CET score.

question: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Pharmacy at PDEA?
answer: Basic eligibility: Education: 10+2 (HSC) or equivalent examination from a recognized board. Mandatory subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology or Mathematics. Minimum marks: General category: At least 45%–50% aggregate marks (varies slightly by institute and rules). Reserved category: At least 40%–45% aggregate marks. Entrance exam requirement: MHT-CET (Maharashtra State Common Entrance Test), NEET (accepted in some institutes), Other approved state/national entrance exams (as per CAP rules). Admission process: Admission is done through Maharashtra CAP (Centralized Admission Process) based on entrance exam score and merit. Additional routes: Direct second-year admission for D.Pharm diploma holders (lateral entry). Eligibility summary: To get B.Pharmacy admission in PDEA, students must complete 12th science with Physics and Chemistry plus Biology/Mathematics, secure minimum required marks, and qualify MHT-CET or equivalent entrance exam.

question: What is the eligibility criteria for BAMS admission at PDEA Ayurveda College?
answer: College: PDEA College of Ayurveda & Research Centre, Pune. Basic eligibility: Education: 10+2 (HSC) or equivalent from a recognized board. Mandatory subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB). Minimum marks: General category: At least 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry and Biology (PCB). Reserved category: At least 40% marks in PCB (as per government rules). Entrance exam requirement: Mandatory: Yes. Exam: NEET-UG (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test). Admission process: Qualify NEET-UG, Register for Maharashtra AYUSH NEET counselling, Fill college preference (PDEA College of Ayurveda & Research Centre), Seat allotment based on NEET rank and category, Document verification and fee payment. Additional requirements: Indian nationality, Completed 10+2 from recognized board, Must participate in centralized counselling (no direct admission). Course details: Course name: BAMS (Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery). Duration: 5.5 years (including 1-year internship). Summary: To get BAMS admission at PDEA Ayurveda College, students must pass 12th with PCB, score at least 50% (40% for reserved category), and qualify NEET-UG followed by Maharashtra AYUSH counselling.

question: Is admission to PDEA engineering college through MHT-CET?
answer: College: PDEA College of Engineering, Pune. Admission through mht cet: Yes. Other accepted exam: JEE Main (Paper 1). Eligibility: Education: 12th (HSC) or equivalent. Mandatory subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics. Admission process: Appear for MHT-CET or JEE Main, Register for CAP (Centralized Admission Process), Fill college and branch preferences, Seat allotment based on merit rank, Document verification and fee payment. Key point: Admission to B.E./B.Tech at PDEA College of Engineering is not direct; it is based on MHT-CET or JEE Main scores through CAP counselling.. Summary: Yes, admission to PDEA engineering college is primarily through MHT-CET or JEE Main followed by CAP counselling.

question: Is admission to PDEA pharmacy college based on MHT-CET scores?
answer: Admission basis: Yes, primarily based on MHT-CET (PCB group) scores. Also considered exam: NEET (in some cases, but MHT-CET is primary for Maharashtra state CAP admissions). Eligibility: Education: 10+2 (HSC) with Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Mathematics. Minimum marks: As per Maharashtra State CET Cell norms (generally 45% general, 40% reserved). Admission process: Appear for MHT-CET (PCB), Apply through Maharashtra CAP (Centralized Admission Process), Seat allotment based on merit rank, Document verification and fee payment. Key point: For B.Pharmacy at PDEA colleges, MHT-CET score is the main criterion for admission through CAP counselling.. Summary: Yes, admission to PDEA pharmacy colleges is mainly through MHT-CET scores followed by CAP counselling.

question: What is the fee structure for B.Tech at PDEA College of Engineering?
answer: College: PDEA College of Engineering, Pune (Manjari, Hadapsar). Program: B.Tech / B.E.. Total course fee range: Minimum: ₹2.64 lakh. Maximum: ₹3.19 lakh. Per year fee estimate: Approximate: ₹80,000 to ₹95,000 per year. Duration: 4 years. Fee components: Tuition fees, Development fees, University fees, Other institutional charges. Notes: Fees may vary slightly based on branch and category, Hostel and mess fees are separate and not included in tuition fee, Fees are subject to revision by the college/university authority. Summary: The total B.Tech fee at PDEA College of Engineering is approximately ₹2.64–₹3.19 lakh for the full 4-year course, excluding hostel and other living expenses.

question: What is the exam schedule for PDEA affiliated colleges?

question: Which university are PDEA colleges affiliated to?

question: How can I check my exam results for PDEA college exams?
answer: Overview: PDEA college exam results are published by Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU) for most courses and can be checked online.. Steps to check results: Visit the official SPPU result portal (results.unipune.ac.in), Select your course (Engineering, MBA, MCA, Arts, Commerce, Science, etc.), Enter your seat number / PRN number, Submit to view your result, Download or print the result for reference. Alternate methods: College notice board (for internal or practical results), Student ERP / college portal (if provided by the institute), Department office for revaluation or photocopy requests. Result details included: Subject-wise marks, Total marks, Grade/CGPA, Result status (Pass/Fail). Revaluation option: Process: Students can apply for revaluation or photocopy of answer sheets through SPPU within the official deadline after result declaration. Summary: PDEA exam results can be checked online through the SPPU result portal using PRN or seat number, along with additional updates from the college ERP or notice board.

question: What is the process for getting transcripts or mark sheets from PDEA colleges?
answer: Overview: Transcripts and mark sheets for PDEA colleges are issued through the college examination section and Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU), depending on the document type.. Mark sheets process: Issuing authority: SPPU + respective PDEA college. Steps: Collect semester-wise mark sheets from the college after result declaration, Verify personal and academic details, Apply at college exam section for corrections if needed. Transcript process: Issuing authority: College examination cell (compiled from SPPU records). Steps: Submit application for transcript at college exam office, Provide required documents (all semester mark sheets, ID proof), Pay prescribed transcript fee, Verification by college and university records, Issue of official transcript (sealed and signed document). Required documents: All semester mark sheets, College ID / PRN number, Identity proof (Aadhaar or passport), Application form, Fee payment receipt. Processing time: Usually 7 to 21 working days depending on verification requirements. Important notes: Transcripts are issued in sealed envelopes for higher studies or abroad applications, Students with pending dues or backlogs may face delays, Corrections must be requested before transcript generation. Summary: Students can obtain mark sheets directly after results, while transcripts must be applied for at the college exam office, verified through SPPU records, and issued after document and fee submission.

question: What are the rules and regulations for students at PDEA colleges?
answer: Overview: PDEA colleges follow university guidelines (SPPU or respective regulatory bodies) along with institutional discipline rules to maintain academic and campus discipline.. Attendance rules: Minimum requirement: Generally 75% attendance is mandatory (as per university norms). Notes: Students with low attendance may not be allowed to appear for exams. Discipline rules: Students must maintain proper behavior inside campus, Ragging is strictly prohibited and punishable under law, Use of mobile phones in classrooms may be restricted, Damage to college property is strictly penalized, Proper dress code may be required in certain departments. Academic rules: Regular submission of assignments and projects is mandatory, Students must follow exam and evaluation guidelines, Cheating or malpractice in exams leads to strict action, Practical and term work completion is compulsory where applicable. Campus conduct: Students must carry ID cards at all times, Smoking, alcohol, and substance abuse are strictly prohibited, Respect for faculty, staff, and peers is mandatory, Participation in academic and co-curricular activities is encouraged. Grievance and compliance: Students can report issues through grievance committees, Anti-ragging committee rules must be strictly followed, Disciplinary action may include warnings, fines, or suspension. Summary: PDEA colleges enforce strict academic and discipline rules including minimum attendance, anti-ragging policies, exam regulations, and general campus conduct guidelines to ensure a safe and structured learning environment.

question: Is there a dress code at PDEA colleges?

question: How do I get a bonafide certificate from a PDEA college?
answer: Overview: A bonafide certificate from PDEA colleges is an official document confirming that a student is currently studying or has studied in the institution.. Application process: Visit the college office or administrative section, Collect and fill the bonafide application form, Submit required details such as name, class, PRN/roll number, and purpose, Attach required documents if asked (ID card, fee receipt), Pay the prescribed processing fee (if applicable), Collect the certificate after verification and approval. Required documents: College ID card, Fee receipt (if requested), Application form, Student details (PRN/Roll number). Processing time: Usually 1 to 3 working days (may vary by college). Where it is used: Scholarship applications, Bank account opening, Passport application, Internships or training programs, Educational verification purposes. Important notes: Bonafide certificates are issued only after verification of student records, Some colleges may also provide an online application option via ERP system, The certificate is typically signed by the principal or authorized officer. Summary: To get a bonafide certificate from a PDEA college, students must apply at the college office or ERP system with basic details and documents, and collect it after verification, usually within a few days.

question: What courses are offered at Prof. Ramkrishna More Arts, Commerce & Science College?
answer: College name: Prof. Ramkrishna More Arts, Commerce & Science College, Akurdi, Pune. Vocational courses: B.Voc in Software Development, Other skill-oriented vocational programs. Streams available: Arts, Commerce, Science, Computer Science & IT, Management, Vocational Studies.
